access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q27.explanation: Câu 27 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 1 chứa căn cứ: "Wars over access to water are a rising possibility and the main conflicts in Africa during the next 25 years could be over this most precious of commodities. “Potential water wars are likely in areas where rivers and lakes are shared by more than one country,” says Mark Evans, a UN worker. Evans predicts: “Population growth and economic development will lead to nearly one in two people in Africa living in countries which face water scarcity or ‘water stress’ within 25 years.” Water scarcity is defined as less than 1,000 cubic metres of water available per person per year, while water stress means less than 1,500 cubic metres of water per person per year.". Lựa chọn LG (theo bảng đáp án) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q27.evidence: Wars over access to water are a rising possibility and the main conflicts in Africa during the next 25 years could be over this most precious of commodities. “Potential water wars are likely in areas where rivers and lakes are shared by more than one country,” says Mark Evans, a UN worker. Evans predicts: “Population growth and economic development will lead to nearly one in two people in Africa living in countries which face water scarcity or ‘water stress’ within 25 years.” Water scarcity is defined as less than 1,000 cubic metres of water available per person per year, while water stress means less than 1,500 cubic metres of water per person per year._

q28.explanation: Câu 28 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 6 chứa căn cứ: "The method of ensuring sufficient water supply, and its management, will increasingly become a political battleground in South Africa. Water Affairs director-general Mike Muller says South Africa is near the end of its dam-building programme. However, there are big projects proposed elsewhere in southern Africa that could possibly be halted by activists who could bring pressure on funding agencies such as the World Bank.". Lựa chọn MM (Mike Muller) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q28.evidence: The method of ensuring sufficient water supply, and its management, will increasingly become a political battleground in South Africa. Water Affairs director-general Mike Muller says South Africa is near the end of its dam-building programme. However, there are big projects proposed elsewhere in southern Africa that could possibly be halted by activists who could bring pressure on funding agencies such as the World Bank._

Đoạn 1 chứa căn cứ: "Wars over access to water are a rising possibility and the main conflicts in Africa during the next 25 years could be over this most precious of commodities. “Potential water wars are likely in areas where rivers and lakes are shared by more than one country,” says Mark Evans, a UN worker. Lựa chọn LG (theo bảng đáp án) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.

q30.explanation: Câu 30 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 3 chứa căn cứ: "One of the UN’s eight millennium development goals is to halve the number of people without sustainable access to safe drinking water by 2020. This had already been agreed upon at the summit, but one of the unresolved issues in the implementation plan was whether the goal on water would be extended to cover sanitation. The risks posed by water-borne diseases in the absence of sanitation facilities mean that the two goals are closely related.". Lựa chọn ME (theo bảng đáp án) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q30.evidence: One of the UN’s eight millennium development goals is to halve the number of people without sustainable access to safe drinking water by 2020. This had already been agreed upon at the summit, but one of the unresolved issues in the implementation plan was whether the goal on water would be extended to cover sanitation. The risks posed by water-borne diseases in the absence of sanitation facilities mean that the two goals are closely related._

q31.explanation: Câu 31 yêu cầu nối thông tin trong câu hỏi với một lựa chọn. Đoạn 4 chứa căn cứ: "Graham Bennetts, a water official in the South African government explains: “Since the 1994 elections, the government has provided easy access to water for 7 million people, but extending this to a further 7 million and ensuring this progress is sustainable is one of South Africa’s foremost implementation challenges.” In South Africa, access to water is defined as 25 litres a person daily that can be accessed from within a distance of 200 metres from where they live. “Although South Africa’s feat far exceeds the UN millennium goal on water supply, severe constraints on local government capacity make a more rapid expansion difficult,” says Bennetts.". Lựa chọn GB (theo bảng đáp án) là lựa chọn khớp với chi tiết này.
q31.evidence: Graham Bennetts, a water official in the South African government explains: “Since the 1994 elections, the government has provided easy access to water for 7 million people, but extending this to a further 7 million and ensuring this progress is sustainable is one of South Africa’s foremost implementation challenges.” In South Africa, access to water is defined as 25 litres a person daily that can be accessed from within a distance of 200 metres from where they live. “Although South Africa’s feat far exceeds the UN millennium goal on water supply, severe constraints on local government capacity make a more rapid expansion difficult,” says Bennetts._
